title: Selenium protects neonates against neurotoxicity from prenatal exposure to manganese.
ehr_evidence: The study used direct measurements of umbilical cord serum concentrations and Neonatal Behavioral Neurological Assessment (NBNA) tests rather than EHR.
summary: An epidemiological study investigating the protective effect of Selenium (Se) against Manganese (Mn)-induced neurotoxicity in neonates in Shanghai, China.
key_findings: Mn was negatively associated with NBNA scores at low Se levels (adjusted OR = 5.7).
The adverse effect of Mn was significantly reduced at high Se levels.
Mn/Se ratio was moderately correlated with NBNA scores.
limitations: Not explicitly stated in the provided text
